amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Mitochondrial biogenesis in S. cerevisiae requires coordinated expression of nuclear and mitochondrial genomes, protein import, membrane assembly, and quality control. Under respiratory growth, the transcription factors Hap2/3/4/5 activate nuclear genes encoding mitochondrial proteins. The RTG (retrograde) pathway senses mitochondrial dysfunction and activates compensatory nuclear gene expression via Rtg1/3 transcription factors. Mitochondrial import involves TOM/TIM complexes for protein translocation.

name: Mitochondrial Biogenesis and Retrograde Signaling
notes: Mitochondrial biogenesis demonstrates organelle-nucleus communication with high AND gate requirements.
scientificAccuracy: Based on extensive research on yeast mitochondrial biogenesis and retrograde signaling.

sources.title: The Rtg1 and Rtg3 proteins are transcription factors required for the expression of the S. cerevisiae genes encoding the peroxisomal proteins citrate synthase and carnitine acetyltransferase.
sources.authors: Liao X, Small WC, Srere PA, Butow RA.

sources.title: Transcriptional activators Hap2 and Hap3 form a complex with a novel nuclear protein, Hap4, that activates respiratory gene expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Retrograde signaling: an elaborate signaling network for the coordination of mitochondrial function and cellular metabolism.
